name: Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Araceli — circular route through the Sierra de Araceli
name_original: Subida al Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Araceli — circular por la Sierra de Araceli
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lucena traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and the distinct terrain of the Subbéticas Natural Park. The region offers a mix of paths, from converted railway lines like the Vía Verde del Aceite to more challenging ascents into the Sierra de Araceli. These running paths provide varied elevation profiles, catering to different fitness levels and preferences for scenic views. Best jogging routes around Lucena The most popular jogging route is Lucena and Sanctuary of Our Lady of Araceli, an 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain towards the sanctuary. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Araceli — circular route through the Sierra de Araceli, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from the Sierra de Araceli. Local runners also love the Sierrezuela-El Mortero Trail, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Jogging around Lucena is defined by olive groves, rolling hills, and the natural features of the Sierra de Araceli. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Lucena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 720 runners have used komoot to explore Lucena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Lucena?
A: The running trails around Lucena offer a diverse landscape, from the rolling hills and extensive olive groves characteristic of the region to the more challenging ascents found in the Sierra de Araceli. You'll find a mix of paths, including converted railway lines like the Vía Verde del Aceite, providing varied elevation profiles for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Lucena?
A: Yes, Lucena offers several routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286068080/via-verde-del-aceite-moriles-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite - Moriles Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option with less elevation gain, making it a good choice for those looking for a more relaxed run. Within Lucena City itself, local parks like Perez Park and Lucena Eco Park provide accessible and relatively flat environments for a leisurely jog.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running paths?
A: Many running paths offer scenic views and pass by interesting natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287190856/laguna-de-los-jarales-circular-by-the-protection-zone-natural-reserve-laguna-de-los-jarales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Jarales — circular by the Protection Zone Natural Reserve Laguna de los Jarales&lt;/a&gt; route takes you around a beautiful lagoon. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4050527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3374591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poljé of the Nava de Cabra&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable natural monuments in the area.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Lucena?
A: Absolutely. Many runners enjoy circular routes for their convenience. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1285750107/ascent-to-the-sanctuary-of-nuestra-senora-de-araceli-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-araceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Araceli — circular route through the Sierra de Araceli&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views and a challenging loop through the Sierra de Araceli.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lucena?
A: The running routes in Lucena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 720 runners have used komoot to explore Lucena's varied terrain. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from olive groves to challenging ascents, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any running trails that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous workout, Lucena offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001033913/lucena-and-sanctuary-of-our-lady-of-araceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lucena and Sanctuary of Our Lady of Araceli&lt;/a&gt; trail, for example, features substantial climbing towards the sanctuary, providing a rewarding challenge and expansive views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Lucena?
A: Lucena City, being in the Philippines, generally experiences a tropical climate. The cooler, drier months from November to May are often preferred for outdoor activities like jogging, as temperatures are more moderate and humidity is lower. However, early mornings or late afternoons are generally the best times to run year-round to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any specific areas or parks within Lucena City suitable for jogging?
A: Within Lucena City, &lt;p&gt;&lt;strong&gt;Perez Park&lt;/strong&gt; is a popular and safe spot for joggers, offering an accessible option in the city center. &lt;strong&gt;Lucena Eco Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provides a tranquil setting with landscaped gardens and trees, ideal for a serene run. Additionally, some residential areas like Calmar Homes and the roads leading to Phirst Park Homes and Metropolis Subd. are favored by local runners.&lt;/p&gt;

Q: Can I find information about local running groups or communities in Lucena?
A: While komoot focuses on routes, local running communities can be a great resource. The Lucena Run Club on Facebook is mentioned as a good resource for discovering local running routes and group runs, which could be helpful for connecting with other runners in the area.

Q: Are there any protected natural areas near Lucena that offer running opportunities?
A: Yes, a bit further afield, the Quezon National Forest Park offers adventurous outdoor pursuits. This protected area features the 'Old Zigzag Road' with steep grades, providing challenging routes for those seeking elevation amidst an intricate system of caves, gorges, springs, and waterfalls. It's also recognized as a bird sanctuary, offering a rich natural backdrop. For more information, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.visitphilippines.org/about-philippines/national-parks/national-parks-in-luzon/quezon-national-park/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quezon National Park page&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are available around Lucena?
A: There are over 70 running routes available around Lucena, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These include 3 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 29 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.
tips: Before reaching the top of the Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Araceli we find this intersection with the road which at a precise moment allows us to see good views of the center called Cristo Marroquí.

This hermitage located in the Sierra de Aras at an altitude of 863 m houses the image of Our Lady of Araceli, patron saint and honorary mayor of Lucena. From here you can see the provinces of Malaga, Granada, Cordoba, Jaen and Seville.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Araceli
